semanticCore: Freezing cold, severe frost, extreme chill, angry glare, or redness of eyes
quranicRelevance: Freezing cold (Zamhareeran - 'la yarawna ... shamsan wa-la zamhareeran').

keyDerivations.meaning: Freezing cold, severe frost, ice chill, or intense winter cold

figurativeOrRareUsages: مُتَّكِئِينَ فِيهَا عَلَى الْأَرَائِكِ ۖ لَا يَرَوْنَ فِيهَا شَمْسًا وَلَا زَمْهَرِيرًا (76:13): Reclining therein on couches. They will not see therein sun (heat) nor freezing cold.
discardedNoise: Zamharah (staring with angry red eyes) - facial expression.
Zamhareeri (hyperbolic slang for very cold weather).
summaryEn: The root represents extreme, freezing cold (Zamhareer), describing the climate of Paradise as perfectly balanced - free from both burning solar heat and painful arctic cold.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى شدة البرد والقر والزمهرير الجليدي وتكشير الأسنان من البرد (زمهر)، واصفاً جو الجنة بالاعتدال فلا شمس محرقة ولا برد مؤلم.
rawText: اِزْمَهَرَّتْ They (the stars) shone, (S, K,) and were intensely bright. (TA.) === It (the eye) became red by reason of anger, (K,) on the occasion of some distressing event; (TA;) as also 🡻زَمْهَرَتْ. (AZ, S, K.) === ازمهرّ It (the face) grinned, so as to display the teeth; or became contracted, with a stern, an austere, or a morose, look. (K.) === It (a day) became intensely cold. (K.) زمهر زَمْهَرَ Q. 1 see the next paragraph. زَمْهَرِيرٌ Intense cold. (S, K.) Such is prepared by God as a punishment for the unbelievers in the latter state. (TA.) In the Kur lxxvi. 13, زَمْهَرِيرًا means Hurting cold: (Bd:) or [simply] cold: (Jel:) or, accord. to some, it there has the meaning next following. (Bd, Jel.) === The moon; (K;) in the dial of Teiyi. (TA.) مُزْمَهِرٌّ Laughing so as to show the teeth: (K:) from the likeness to the shining of stars. (TA.) === Angry: (K:) or violently angry. (S.)
